mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS reflects an active Fed tightening cycle that raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.91% in Sep 2018 up from sub-1% levels in 2017 reflecting ongoing hiking cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS uptrends are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month after a period of sustained lows, it signals an abrupt labor-market inflection that triggers rapid risk-off repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2pp relative to prior month reading
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% in Aug 2018, near multi-decade lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with historically low unemployment
failure_condition: In triggered instances, SPX 5-day forward returns are positive more than 55% of the time, or Rank IC < 0.02 across triggered samples over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deceleration in INDPRO growth and PAYEMS growth over a rolling 3-month window signals broad-based real-economy slowdown that anticipates earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. Neither series alone is sufficient; the effect requires simultaneous weakening of production and employment.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 108.23 and PAYEMS at 149,279k as of Aug 2018, both near cycle highs
regime_hint: any regime with concurrent supply and labor deceleration
failure_condition: Joint deceleration episodes fail to produce negative mean 60-day forward returns relative to unconditional baseline (difference not significant at p > 0.10), or interaction term adds no explanatory power versus single-series models (delta Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion with elevated policy rates, a month-over-month decline in HOUST signals that higher borrowing costs are curbing interest-rate-sensitive housing demand, foreshadowing broader consumption weakness and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This effect is specific to late-cycle regimes and does not hold in early-cycle recoveries where housing declines can reflect supply constraints rather than demand destruction.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1168k units in Jul 2018 while FEDFUNDS at 1.91% amid ongoing hikes
failure_condition: HOUST declines during late-cycle tightening periods are not followed by significantly negative 20-day SPX forward returns (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign when tested against early-cycle housing declines used as placebo.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds 2.5%, it raises the probability of accelerated Fed tightening beyond market expectations, increasing discount-rate uncertainty and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 251.846 in Aug 2018 implying YoY inflation near or above 2.5%
regime_hint: rising inflation with active monetary tightening
failure_condition: Triggered high-inflation episodes fail to show significantly negative mean 20-day forward returns versus baseline (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent positive trend in PAYEMS month-over-month growth reflects sustained labor income generation supporting consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 149,279k in Aug 2018 continuing multi-year uptrend
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS trend and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive PAYEMS trend periods do not outperform negative trend periods at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Strong RSAFS growth occurring even as FEDFUNDS rises indicates consumer demand resilience that offsets tightening headwinds, signaling durable earnings strength and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal requires the joint condition of rising rates and rising retail sales; either alone is uninformative or offsetting.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 509,025 million in Aug 2018 rising alongside FEDFUNDS at 1.91%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with resilient consumer demand
failure_condition: Joint high-RSAFS-growth/rising-FEDFUNDS episodes fail to outperform unconditional 20-day forward return baseline (p > 0.10), or interaction term shows no incremental Rank IC over single-series RSAFS trend model (delta < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle high-interest-rate regime, a month-over-month deceleration in INDPRO signals that tighter financial conditions are beginning to constrain industrial output, prompting rapid short-horizon de-risking and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. This mechanism is regime-specific and does not hold when rates are low or falling, where INDPRO deceleration more often reflects transitory supply-chain noise.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change turns negative while FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 108.23 and FEDFUNDS at 1.91% in Sep 2018 snapshot
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion under elevated policy rates
failure_condition: Triggered INDPRO deceleration events under high-rate regime fail to produce significantly negative 5-day forward SPX returns (p > 0.05), or effect is indistinguishable from low-rate regime placebo test.
